source_jobs.title: In-house content creator
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT

NewForm is the creative intelligence company. We help category disruptors like Kalshi, ElevenLabs, Babbel, and others scale paid ads by turning creative into a measurable, repeatable growth system.

We oversee $100M+ per year in paid media spend and create thousands of human ads each month, with each ad making our system smarter.



THE ROLE

As an in-house content creator, you will be responsible for executing the vision set by creative strategists. You will perform both direct scripts and improvise concepts sent to you by the creative strategy team. This role is ideal for someone who can be both talent (on-camera) and producer, creating content that feels native, converts, and can be produced at scale.

RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Create dozens of short-form video ads a day

 - Be able to learn the ins and outs of repeatable creative formats (e.g., fake podcast, different types of skits, street interviews, etc)

 - Collaborate with the creative strategy team to interpret results and ship iterations (new hooks, angles, openings, pacing, CTAs, thumbnails).

 - Maintain a content pipeline: content calendar, shot lists, asset management, and versioning.

 - Stay current on platform trends, creator formats, audio, editing styles, and best practices—then translate them into winning assets.

 - Ensure content aligns with brand voice, product claims, and compliance guidelines.

REQUIREMENTS

 - MUST have hands-on experience with being on camera (ideally ads made for paid social)

 - Strong communication and organizational skills

 - A sharp eye for what makes a winning paid ad creative

 - Ability to manage creative production workflows

BENEFITS

 - This is a part-time in-office position (NYC). Employees are expected to be in office 1-3 days/week.

 - Competitive compensation (30-100/hr).

 - Competitive bonus (top creators earn 1k/mo in bonuses)

 - Get to be the face of some of the fastest-growing companies in the world

 - Clear pipeline to a full-time creative strategist role with higher compensation

source_jobs.title: Video Editor (UGC)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT

NewForm is the creative intelligence company. We help category disruptors like Kalshi, ElevenLabs, Babbel, and others scale paid ads by turning creative into a measurable, repeatable growth system.

We oversee $100M+ per year in paid media spend and create thousands of human ads each month, with each ad making our system smarter.

 

THE ROLE

As a Video Editor, you’ll be responsible for turning raw footage, creator content, and scripts into high-performing short-form ads for paid social. You’ll work closely with creative strategists, producers, and media buyers to build scroll-stopping, conversion-focused videos. This role is ideal for someone who understands pacing, hooks, retention curves, and how to edit for performance. You’ll be producing at high volume, iterating quickly based on performance data, and directly influencing account scale through your edits.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Edit short-form paid social ads (UGC, podcast ads, product demos, street interviews, etc.)

 - Produce multiple variations per concept (different hooks, pacing, overlays, CTAs)

 - Optimize videos for platform specs (Meta, TikTok, YouTube Shorts)

 - Iterate on edits based on retention data, CPA, and performance feedback

 - Collaborate with creative strategists to refine messaging and structure

 - Organize and manage project files for scalable production workflows

 - Stay current on short-form trends, editing styles, and paid social best practices


REQUIREMENTS

 - 2–5 years of video editing experience (agency experience is highly preferred)

 - Strong portfolio showcasing short-form paid social ads

 - Proficiency in Adobe Premiere Pro or capcut (required)

 - Experience with After Effects for motion graphics (preferred)

 - Deep understanding of pacing, retention mechanics, and conversion-focused editing

 - Ability to edit quickly without sacrificing clarity or quality

 - Strong organizational skills and attention to detail

 - (Must) Experience editing ads for DTC, mobile apps, or SaaS

 - (Must) Familiarity with Meta and TikTok ad formats, ratios, and specs


BENEFITS

 - Full-time, remote role

 - Competitive compensation based on experience (12k-36k/yr)

 - Performance-based bonus

 - Annual team offsites (previous trips include Tulum, Mexico and Hokkaido, Japan)

source_jobs.title: Performance Creative Specialist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT

NewForm is the creative intelligence company. We help category disruptors like Kalshi, ElevenLabs, Babbel, and others scale paid ads by turning creative into a measurable, repeatable growth system.

We oversee $100M+ per year in paid media spend and create thousands of human ads each month, with each ad making our system smarter.



THE ROLE

As a Performance Creative Specialist, you will help drive the creative operation from A to Z by supporting the development and execution of high-performing paid social creative. You'll work closely with our Creative Strategists to strategize, concept, script, and execute ad concepts by leveraging our in-house content team and external network of creators.

You will play a key role in connecting performance data to creative decisions. This role requires a strong eye for what makes winning creative, paired with the analytical ability to identify patterns, extract insights from performance data, and translate those insights into stronger creative strategies and executions.



RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Develop paid social ad concepts, hooks, angles, and scripts alongside the Creative Strategy team

 - Support creative strategy by generating variations and new concepts based on performance insights

 - Partner closely with editors, creators, and the production team to bring ad concepts to life

 - Analyze creative performance data to identify winning patterns, uncover insights, and improve future concepts

 - Stay deeply tapped into organic and paid social and creative trends to continuously improve creative output

 - Help maintain organized creative workflows, timelines, and feedback loops across the production process



REQUIREMENTS

 - Analytical by nature, with the ability to identify meaningful signals from large datasets and performance trends

 - Strong communication and organizational skills

 - Deep knowledge of cultural

 - A sharp eye for what makes winning ad creative

 - Excellent copywriting and ad scripting skills

 - Ability to manage creative production workflows and timelines

 - Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative environment




BENEFITS

 - This is a full-time remote position

 - Competitive compensation ($30k - $60k based salary per year)

 - Performance-based bonus structure

 - Opportunity for compensation reviews based on performance

source_jobs.title: Director, Growth Marketing
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT

NewForm is the creative intelligence company. We help category disruptors like Kalshi, ElevenLabs, Babbel, and others scale paid ads by turning creative into a measurable, repeatable growth system.

 

We oversee $100M+ per year in paid media spend and create thousands of human ads each month, with each ad making our system smarter.

 

THE ROLE

As a Director of Growth Marketing, you will play a key role in leading client relationships, media strategy, and creative performance across several high-growth accounts. You will oversee forward-deployed creatives and media buyers to drive efficient spend, scale accounts, and connect media strategy with creative intelligence.

This role is primarily client-facing and strategic, but you should also be able to dive into the weeds. You will use your platform expertise, analytical judgment, and understanding of creative performance to help clients grow faster and more efficiently.

RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Oversee annual media budgets of $5M–$50M across channels, primarily Meta, TikTok, and Google

 - Lead client relationships and communication, including regular meetings and QBRs

 - Develop cohesive growth strategies with creative at the center of the paid ads program

 - Translate creative performance data into actionable strategy for clients, creative teams, and media buyers

 - Proactively identify optimizations to improve media efficiency and scalability, including work outside the dashboard such as funnel strategy, signal quality, offer testing, and measurement

 - Partner with forward-deployed creatives and media buyers to turn insights into high-performing content and scaled spend

REQUIREMENTS

 - 4–10 years of paid media experience, with agency and/or consumer app experience highly preferred

 - Deep expertise in Meta required

 - Experience managing accounts with seven-figure annual media budgets

 - Strong presentation skills, including the ability to craft compelling decks and communicate clearly in client meetings

 - Strong analytical skills and comfort connecting performance data to strategic recommendations

 - Preferred: deep understanding of mobile advertising, including SKAN, AEM, MMPs, and related measurement systems

BENEFITS

 - Full-time, in-office position in New York City

 - Competitive compensation: $130k–$175k base salary

 - Competitive bonus structure, with total compensation up to double base (OTE 200k+)

 - Company-paid health, dental, and vision insurance

 - $300/month commuter benefit

 - Annual team offsites. Our last two were in Tulum, Mexico and Hokkaido, Japan.

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
NewForm is building the creative intelligence layer for paid advertising. We pair an in-house creative pod with Framework, our proprietary statistical testing engine that instruments every ad it ships, to build a creative ontology for each brand: a living map of what works, why it works, and how to make more of it. The service we sell is the creative your paid spend needs. The asset we build is yours to keep.

We work with brands like Kalshi, Babbel, Acorns, and Western Union. We oversee north of $100M in annual ad spend, ship 3,000+ ads a month, and Framework has run 6,000+ structured creative tests to date. We're at $7M ARR, doubling YoY for two years running.




THE ROLE

This is a Palantir-style Forward-Deployed Engineer role adapted for the creative supply chain. You won't be writing tickets handed to you by a PM. You'll be in the room when a strategist hits a workflow ceiling, deciding in real time whether the answer is a new Framework feature, a one-off automation, or a data pipeline that didn't exist yesterday. Then you build it.

You'll embed with our services pods: strategists, creators, media buyers, Creative Intelligence Leads and build the software they need to run faster, learn more, and ship more winning ads. You own a roadmap, not a ticket queue. The roadmap is generated by what you observe sitting next to operators in real client work.

The work compounds in two directions. The bespoke things you ship for one client become the next generalized Framework feature. The patterns you spot across the services team become the roadmap.





WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Embed with one or two pods at a time. Sit in their reviews, briefs, and post-mortems. Understand the work end to end before you write code.

 - Build into Framework when the problem is structural. Build bespoke when the problem is one client's. Make the call on which is which.

 - Own the end-to-end technical lifecycle for client deployments: onboarding, ad account integrations (Meta, TikTok, Google), data reconciliation, custom dashboards, and the tooling that closes the gap between platform APIs and how strategists actually want to work.

 - Translate field signal into product. Every workaround a strategist invents is a feature waiting to be written. Surface those patterns to the core engineering team and to leadership directly.

 - Run your own roadmap. We'll set direction; you set sequence. You should be able to defend why the thing you shipped this week mattered more than the thing you didn't.



WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - 30 days: You've embedded with at least one pod, mapped where their workflow is breaking, and shipped your first piece of useful tooling to production. Small is fine. Real is what matters.

 - 60 days: You've shipped meaningful client tooling end to end, identified two or three patterns that should become Framework features, and the pod you're embedded with treats you as part of the team.

 - 6 months: You've shipped at least one of those patterns into core Framework, built repeatable deployment runbooks for new client onboardings, and the services team is asking for you by name. The playbook for the next FDE hire is yours.
   


WHO YOU ARE

 - 3+ years building software, with real time in client-facing, deployment, or solutions engineering work. You've shipped things that lived inside someone else's company.

 - Fluent in TypeScript and Next.js, comfortable in modern React, and have built non-trivial backends.

 - You've worked with LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini) at the application layer.

 - You can hold a real conversation with a non-technical operator without losing precision. You can sit in a media review and understand what's actually broken. You can also write the PR that fixes it.

 - You're scrappy in the way that ships, not the way that accumulates tech debt. The thing you build for a client today should still be running cleanly six months from now.

 - You're comfortable with ambiguity. Client environments differ. Specs

source_jobs.title: Business Development Intern
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT

NewForm is the creative intelligence company. We help category disruptors like Kalshi, ElevenLabs, Babbel, and others scale paid ads by turning creative into a measurable, repeatable growth system.

We oversee $100M+ per year in paid media spend and create thousands of human ads each month, with each ad making our system smarter.



THE ROLE

As a Business Development Intern, you'll work directly with Newform's leadership and sales team to support outbound sales, partnerships, and revenue expansion. This role is ideal for someone who wants hands-on exposure to how a high-growth agency acquires, qualifies, and closes clients. You'll help research prospects, support outreach efforts, prepare sales materials, and assist in managing the top of the funnel. This is not a "watch from the sidelines" internship—you'll be embedded in real deals with billion dollar companies.



RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Research and identify potential clients across tech, mobile, and consumer brands

 - Build and maintain prospect lists using tools like LinkedIn, Apollo, HubSpot

 - Support outbound outreach efforts (email, LinkedIn, and warm intros)

 - Help prepare pitch decks, proposals, and sales materials

 - Assist in tracking leads, pipeline status, and deal notes in CRM tools

 - Sit in on sales calls and client meetings to learn discovery, positioning, and closing

 - Support partnerships, referrals, and special growth initiatives as needed


REQUIREMENTS

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills

 - Highly organized with strong attention to detail

 - Comfortable working with data, spreadsheets, and basic analysis

 - Curious about startups, marketing, sales, and entrepreneurship

 - Self-starter mindset: proactive, reliable, and eager to learn

 - (Preferred) Prior experience in sales, marketing, consulting, or startups

 - (Preferred) Familiarity with paid social, digital marketing, or creator-led brands


BENEFITS

 - Paid internship

 - Role with office in NYC

 - Direct exposure to agency leadership and real client deals

 - Hands-on experience in business development and growth strategy

 - Opportunity for a full-time role upon successful completion

 - Fast-paced environment with meaningful responsibility

 - Annual team offsites (previous trips include Tulum, Mexico and Hokkaido, Japan)

source_jobs.title: Full stack engineer
source_jobs.description:
About

NewForm is building marketing tools for the new age of advertising. Most marketing analytics products are audience-focused and have become obsolete as platforms like Meta, Tiktok, etc move to a content-first philosophy. We are first and foremost a tech-enabled service, deploying our proprietary technology and service in tandem to drive outcomes for our clients.

Our Products

Our AI-powered suite of tools includes:

 - Framework: A proprietary statistical engine to test creatives 30% more efficiently than manual testing, saving budget and accelerating the finding of winners

 - WillBot: An AI-powered Slack chatbot that keeps teams connected to their marketing metrics in real-time

 - More: Custom-built solutions for reporting, dashboards, and media buying automation

Our Impact

We've partnered with innovative brands across industries, including Acorns, Bluechew, Found, Coffee Meets Bagel, RentApp, Western Union and more.

Our Advantage

What sets us apart is our unique combination of deep marketing expertise and cutting-edge AI technology. We are in the arena applying years of hands-on marketing experience to create solutions that deliver real-world results. Plus, we leverage a large list of existing client relationships to build more quickly and effectively.

What you'll do in this role

 - You'll take ownership of projects from code to infrastructure. We're looking for someone who has set up production systems before and isn't afraid to build and deploy products from scratch.

 - You will operate autonomously, working closely with leadership to define product roadmap and deploy new feaures. We don't have product managers. You will be speaking to the internal team and our clients directly, building things that actually move the needle.

 - You're a full-stack engineer with robust LLM, Node/Typescript and React (Next.js) experience and a willingness to learn. Our current stack consists of a Typescript backend, Next.js frontend, and LLM API based chat and classifiers. As our product grows, you'll be ready to tackle challenges wherever they might come up. If a project requires working with something you're not familiar with, you'll take time to learn a new technology.

Who you are

 - You have 3+ years of experience as a software engineer.

 - You're experienced with LLMs, Typescript & Next.js. You're familiar with building complex Typescript/Node backends, as well as with building modern React frontends that handle complex data visualizations. You also have some familiarity with leveraging LLM APIs like OpenAI, Anthropic and/or Gemini to build chatbots.

 - You're a full-stack generalist who loves a good challenge. You can build features end-to-end and reliably ship well-tested code, with an eye towards maintainability.

 - You love learning new technologies. Whether it's working with the newest o1 reasoning language model APIs, setting up Voice to Voice LLMs or interacting with a brand new APIs from partners, you’re excited about learning something new and quickly pick it up.

 - You have an entrepreneurial streak and/or demonstrated history of technical leadership. You've done cool things in the past, both at work and outside the office.

Benefits

 - This is a full-time in-office position (NYC). Engineers are expected to be in office 3-4 days/week.

 - Competitive compensation (100-180k/yr base)

 - Competitive equity (.25%-1% at a company doubling YoY for the last two years)

 - Company-paid health, dental, and vision insurance

 - 300/mo commuter benefit

 - Annual team offsites (last two were Tulum, Mexico and Hokkaido, Japan)

Work Authorization Requirement
Candidates must be authorized to work in the United States without employer sponsorship, now or in the future. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this role, including in the future.

source_jobs.title: In-House CPA/Accountant
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT NEWFORM

NewForm is the creative intelligence company. We help category disruptors like Kalshi, ElevenLabs, Babbel, and others scale paid ads by turning creative into a measurable, repeatable growth system.

We oversee $100M+ per year in paid media spend and create thousands of human ads each month, with each ad making our system smarter.





THE ROLE

We’re looking for a proactive and organized Accountant who can help manage and support the financial operations of a growing performance marketing agency.

This role goes beyond basic bookkeeping, you’ll play an important role in maintaining accurate financial records, supporting reporting processes, improving accounting workflows, and helping leadership maintain financial visibility across the business.

You’ll work closely with internal stakeholders in a fast-paced remote environment where accuracy, ownership, and reliability are highly valued.



RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Manage day-to-day accounting operations, including accounts payable and accounts receivable

 - Prepare monthly financial reports and maintain accurate financial records

 - Perform bank, credit card, and account reconciliations

 - Maintain and organize financial data within QuickBooks

 - Track company expenses, invoices, and financial documentation

 - Support internal reporting and financial visibility for leadership

 - Ensure accuracy and consistency across accounting records and financial processes

 - Assist in improving accounting workflows and operational efficiency as the company scales
   


REQUIREMENTS

 - Must be a Certified Public Accountant (CPA)

 - Must have at least 2–3 years of experience working with US based clients or companies

 - Strong experience with QuickBooks (certification is a plus)

 - Strong understanding of accounting principles and financial reporting

 - Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills

 - Ability to work independently and manage deadlines in a remote environment

 - Strong communication and documentation skills

 - Comfortable working in a fast-paced, high-growth company
   


COMPENSATION & WORK SETUP

 - 100% remote work setup

 - Competitive compensation package

 - Opportunity to work with a fast-growing global company

 - Work alongside a high-performing team supporting leading international brands

 - Long-term growth opportunities within a rapidly scaling organization

source_jobs.title: Content Producer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT

NewForm is the creative intelligence company. We help category disruptors like Kalshi, ElevenLabs, Babbel, and others scale paid ads by turning creative into a measurable, repeatable growth system.

We oversee $100M+ per year in paid media spend and create thousands of human ads each month, with each ad making our system smarter.


 


THE ROLE

You're a content creator at your core. You know how to make things that stop the scroll, you live on social media, and you have a real body of work that proves it. What sets you apart is that you can also run a proper set: you understand lighting, exposure, and how to get the most out of talent on camera, whether shooting with a phone or Sony FX30 cameras.

This is an impactful role at a high-growth company pioneering what performance creative looks like. As we scale our production output, we're investing in the people who can lead sets, build systems, and train the next wave of production talent around them.



RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Produce and shoot content across formats — from phone-first run-and-gun to fully lit multi-camera setups — matching the approach to the creative brief at a high volume daily.

 - Own on-set production from prep through wrap, including scheduling, logistics, location scouting, and set dressing.

 - Operate and set up camera, lighting, and audio with precision. You can run a multi-camera DSLR setup with Sony cameras (FX30 or similar) and dial in exposures, color, and lighting to look great on screen.

 - Direct and coach creator talent to get their best performances on camera.

 - Train and lead junior production staff and interns to execute a high volume of shoots every day.

 - Work closely with Creative Strategists to translate scripts into production-ready plans.

 - Stay on top of what's trending on social — formats, creators, platform behavior — and bring that awareness into every shoot.

 - Tap your large network of NYC creators to recruit great talent for shoots.

 - Be comfortable stepping in front of the camera yourself if the moment calls for it.

 - Partner with editors post-shoot to hand off footage cleanly and with clear context.

 - Maintain production equipment, including inventory, upkeep, and day-to-day readiness.

 


REQUIREMENTS

 - An active content creator with a real body of work you're proud to share

 - 2+ years of hands-on production experience across social content formats, including phone and DSLR cameras

 - Solid DP fundamentals — you understand exposure, color, composition, and lighting at a technical level and can execute them on set.

 - Technically proficient with Sony cameras (FX30 and similar) and equally comfortable shooting on a phone.

 - Experience running and dressing sets; you know how to make a space look intentional, not accidental.

 - Comfortable directing talent of all experience levels.

 - An avid social media consumer with genuine cultural fluency — you know what's trending and why it works.

 - Organized and able to manage multiple concurrent shoots without dropping anything.

 - Calm and solutions-oriented when things don't go as planned.

 - A large network of NYC creators you can activate — real relationships, not just a following.

 - (Preferred) Experience producing content for paid social or performance marketing.
   
    


BENEFITS

 - This is a full-time, in-office position based in NYC.

 - Competitive compensation ($62,000–$75,000 base salary)

 - Company-paid health, dental, and vision insurance

 - $300/month commuter benefit

 - Annual team offsites (our last two were in Tulum, Mexico and Hokkaido, Japan)

source_jobs.title: GTM, Founding AE
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT
NewForm is the creative intelligence company. We help category disruptors like Kalshi, ElevenLabs, Babbel, and others scale paid ads by turning creative into a measurable, repeatable growth system.

We oversee $100M+ per year in paid media spend and create thousands of human ads each month, with each ad making our system smarter.

THE ROLE
We're hiring a Founding Account Executive to own and scale the next phase of revenue at NewForm. We've built a strong sales foundation and a growing pipeline across enterprise teams and mid-market disruptors. Now we need someone to take ownership of that opportunity set and convert it into revenue across both Framework (our software platform) and our managed creative service.



This is a founding GTM role. You'll work directly with the founders, a team of BDRs driving outbound pipeline, and our Forward Deployed creatives and engineers to determine the best solution for each client, whether that's software, services, or both. Because we're outcomes-based, the sale is consultative by nature: you're scoping what will actually move a client's numbers and structuring the deal around it.


You'll sit across the table from top-tier management teams at world-class consumer and prosumer companies. You'll field inbound from NewForm's broader network of VCs, PE firms, consultants, publishers, and partners, and you'll help define the playbook, positioning, and process the rest of the sales org is built on.
For the right person, this role offers very high upside, broad exposure across the business, and a clear path to sales leadership as the team expands.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the sales process from qualified opportunity through close

 - Sell across Framework and our managed service offering, working with Forward Deployed creatives and engineers to scope the right solution for each client

 - Structure outcomes-based engagements, pricing and packaging around the results we drive

 - Take ownership of a growing pipeline across enterprise and mid-market opportunities

 - Work closely with a team of BDRs to convert outbound pipeline into qualified opportunities and closed revenue

 - Partner with sales ops to maintain strong pipeline hygiene, process rigor, and deal follow-through

 - Field and run inbound, including opportunities from NewForm's network of investors, consultants, publishers, and partners

 - Run discovery, manage follow-ups, drive urgency, and quarterback complex deals across multiple stakeholders

 - Partner directly with founders on deal strategy, positioning, pricing, and packaging

 - Build the founding GTM playbook: sales process, pipeline management, and core operating rhythms

 - Develop a strong point of view on our market, our buyers, and where our software and services resonate most

 - Grow into sales leadership as the organization expands



WHO YOU ARE

 - Based in NYC and excited to work in person with a fast-moving team (3/5 days in office)

 - Experienced in founding roles or early-stage GTM efforts

 - Have 3+ years owning a quota and consistently hitting or exceeding pipeline and/or revenue targets

 - Comfortable selling in a dynamic environment across outbound, inbound, software, and services

 - Able to hold your own with senior management teams at world-class consumer and prosumer companies

 - Know how to work cross-functionally with BDRs, Forward Deployed teams, sales ops, and leadership to move deals forward

 - Commercially sharp and able to sell consultatively to sophisticated buyers

 - Motivated by upside, pace, and the chance to earn outsized responsibility quickly



NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience in media, technology, agency, SaaS, or adjacent environments

 - Strong plus: mobile, performance marketing, or advertising experience

 - Experience selling more than one product type, or navigating both services and software conversations

 - Familiarity with startup sales environments and helping build a sales org from an early stage
   
   

WHY THIS ROLE

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Creative
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT

NewForm is the creative intelligence company. We help category disruptors like Kalshi, ElevenLabs, Babbel, and others scale paid ads by turning creative into a measurable, repeatable growth system.

We oversee $100M+ per year in paid media spend and create thousands of human ads each month, with each ad making our system smarter.





THE ROLE

As a Forward Deployed Creative (FDC), you will lead the creative operation from A to Z for your assigned clients. You will be responsible for strategizing, concepting, and executing paid ad concepts by leveraging our in-house content team and external network of creators.

As a Forward Deployed Creative (FDC), you are responsible for connecting the data to the creative. You have a strong eye for what makes winning creative, while also being analytical and using performance data to inform your strategy and execution.



RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Serve as the primary creative point of contact for assigned clients

 - Lead ad scripting, paid media content strategy, and creator execution

 - Partner closely with editors to bring ad concepts to life

 - Manage multiple clients and creative projects simultaneously with precision

 - Analyze performance data to identify insights, improve content performance, and scale winning concepts

 - Stay ahead of paid social and creative trends to keep clients on the cutting edge




REQUIREMENTS

 - Analytical by nature, with the ability to identify meaningful signals from large datasets and performance trends

 - Strong communication and organizational skills

 - A sharp eye for what makes a winning ad creative

 - Ability to manage creative production workflows and timelines
   
   

We encourage applicants to apply even if they do not meet every requirement. We are a team of industry outsiders disrupting the way growth is done, and we value people from diverse backgrounds who are eager to learn and grow.




BENEFITS

 - This is a full-time, in-office position based in NYC. Forward Deployed Creatives (FDCs) are expected to be in the office 5 days a week.

 - Competitive compensation ($70,000–$110,000 base salary)

 - Competitive bonus structure (top Forward Deployed Creatives (FDCs) earn 30%+ annual bonuses)

 - Company-paid health, dental, and vision insurance

 - $300/month commuter benefit

 - Annual team offsites (our last two were in Tulum, Mexico and Hokkaido, Japan)

source_jobs.title: Sales Operations Specialist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT

NewForm is the creative intelligence company. We help category disruptors like Kalshi, ElevenLabs, Babbel, and others scale paid ads by turning creative into a measurable, repeatable growth system.

We oversee $100M+ per year in paid media spend and create thousands of human ads each month, with each ad making our system smarter.



THE ROLE

As a Sales Operations Specialist, you'll sit at the center of our revenue engine - working closely with sales, leadership, and delivery to drive pipeline visibility, deal execution, and overall sales efficiency.

Your role is both executional and systems-oriented. You'll help manage proposals, CRM hygiene, and day-to-day sales workflows, while also improving the underlying systems and processes that help us move faster and close better.

This role is ideal for someone who is detail-oriented, systems-minded, and wants to work close to deals and revenue. You'll partner closely with business development and leadership to reduce friction in the sales process and ensure we have clear visibility into pipeline and performance.



RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own and maintain the sales CRM (HubSpot, Salesforce, or equivalent) as the source of truth for pipeline and revenue

 - Ensure leads, deals, and pipeline stages are accurate, up to date, and reflective of real deal progress

 - Track inbound and outbound leads and support proper routing, enrichment, and follow-up

 - Support proposal and sales material development (decks, case studies, scopes), ensuring consistency and speed in deal execution

 - Assist in building and improving sales processes, workflows, and automations across tools (HubSpot, Clay, Zapier, etc.)

 - Generate weekly and monthly sales reporting for leadership (pipeline, conversion rates, cycle length, deal velocity)

 - Coordinate handoff from sales to account teams to ensure smooth client onboarding

 - Support forecasting, quota tracking, and performance analysis

 - Identify operational gaps in the sales process and proactively suggest improvements



REQUIREMENTS

 - 2–5 years of experience in sales operations, revenue operations, or a related role

 - Experience in a B2B agency, GTM team, or high-growth startup preferred

 - Strong organizational and process management skills

 - Comfortable working with CRMs, spreadsheets, and dashboards

 - High attention to detail—you care about clean data and clear systems

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills

 - Analytical mindset with the ability to turn data into insights

 - Ability to manage multiple workstreams in a fast-paced environment

 - (Must) Familiarity with HubSpot, Salesforce, or similar CRM tools

 - (Nice to have) Experience with tools like Clay, Zapier, or similar automation platforms



BENEFITS

 - Full-time remote role based in the Philippines

 - Competitive compensation based on experience/geo (12k-60k/yr)

 - Performance-based bonus

 - Annual team offsites (previous trips include Tulum, Mexico and Hokkaido, Japan)

source_jobs.title: Growth Analyst
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT

NewForm is the creative intelligence company. We help category disruptors like Kalshi, ElevenLabs, Babbel, and others scale paid ads by turning creative into a measurable, repeatable growth system.

We oversee $100M+ per year in paid media spend and create thousands of human ads each month, with each ad making our system smarter.



THE ROLE

As a Growth Analyst, you will lead the creative operation from A->Z for your assigned clients. You will be responsible for strategizing, concepting, and executing on paid ad concepts, leveraging our in-house content team and external network of creators. As a Growth Analyst, you are responsible for connecting the data to the creative. You have an eye for what creative is good, but also are analytical and use data to inform your strategy and execution.



RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Serve as the main point of contact on creative for assigned clients

 - Lead ad scripting, paid media content strategy, and execution with creators

 - Partner with editors to bring ads to life

 - Manage multiple clients and projects simultaneously with precision

 - Analyze performance data to continuously improve content performance and scale

 - Stay ahead of paid ads trends to keep clients on the cutting edge



REQUIREMENTS

 - Analytical in nature, ability to look at large swaths of data/trends and gather signal

 - Strong communication and organizational skills

 - A sharp eye for what makes a winning ad creative

 - Ability to manage creative production workflows

We encourage applicants to apply even if they don't meet all of the requirements. We are a group of industry outsiders disrupting the way growth is done, so we value those with different backgrounds who are eager to learn.



BENEFITS

 - This is a full-time in-office position (NYC). Strategists are expected to be in office 3-4 days/week

 - Competitive compensation (70-110k/yr base)

 - Competitive bonus (top analysts earn 30%+ bonuses)

 - Company-paid health, dental, and vision insurance

 - 300/mo commuter benefit

 - Annual team offsites (last two were Tulum, Mexico and Hokkaido, Japan)

ABOUT

NewForm is the creative intelligence company. We help category disruptors like Kalshi, ElevenLabs, Babbel, and others scale paid ads by turning creative into a measurable, repeatable growth system.

We oversee $100M+ per year in paid media spend and create thousands of human ads each month, with each ad making our system smarter.


THE ROLE

As a playable ads editor, you will be responsible for executing the vision set by creative strategist. You will be responsible for turning game or app concepts into HTML5/Unity/Cocos-based interactive ad creatives, from prototype to final build, optimized for performance, engagement, and ad network requirements.

RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build interactive playable ads using Unity or other similar tools

 - Create simple gameplay mechanics strictly for ad purposes

 - Optimize playables for performance and ad network requirements

 - Collaborate with designers and US-based creative strategists

REQUIREMENTS

 - MUST have hands-on experience with creating playable ads

 - Strong traditional editing skills

 - Strong communication and organizational skills

 - A sharp eye for what makes a winning paid ad creative

 - Ability to manage creative production workflows

BENEFITS

 - This is a a full time remote position.

 - Competitive compensation based on geo.

 - Competitive bonus (top editors earn 500/mo in bonuses)

source_jobs.title: Creative Production Intern
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT

NewForm is the creative intelligence company. We help category disruptors like Kalshi, ElevenLabs, Babbel, and others scale paid ads by turning creative into a measurable, repeatable growth system.

We oversee $100M+ per year in paid media spend and create thousands of human ads each month, with each ad making our system smarter.



THE ROLE

As a Creative Producer Intern at NewForm, you’ll support the production team in bringing creative concepts to life — from pre-production planning to on-set execution. While Creative Strategists develop the ideas and scripts, you’ll help ensure shoots run smoothly, content gets captured efficiently, talent feels welcomed, and every production stays organized and on track.

This is a hands-on opportunity for someone excited about content creation, production, and social-first storytelling. You’ll gain direct experience working on paid social campaigns, creator shoots, podcast-style productions, and fast-paced creative operations.



RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Assist with coordinating shoot logistics, scheduling, locations, and production prep

 - Support on-set production for UGC, podcast, lifestyle, and social content shoots

 - Help set up and organize camera, lighting, and audio equipment

 - Work alongside Creative Strategists and our Creative Producer to execute creative briefs

 - Assist with creator and talent management during shoots

 - Help source props, wardrobe, and production materials as needed

 - Ensure footage, assets, and files are properly organized post-shoot

 - Support production operations, equipment organization, and studio upkeep

 - Help maintain smooth communication across creators, editors, and production team members

 - Be proactive in solving on-set challenges and supporting the team wherever needed



REQUIREMENTS

 - Strong interest in content production, filmmaking, social media, or creative marketing

 - Organized, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple moving pieces

 - Comfortable working in a fast-paced, collaborative environment

 - Positive attitude and willingness to learn on set

 - Familiarity with cameras, lighting, audio equipment, and/or editing tools

 - Interest in short-form content, creators, and paid social marketing

 - Previous internship, freelance, student film, or creator experience is a plus but not required



BENEFITS

 - Paid internship

 - Hands-on experience working with a high-performing creative and marketing team

 - Exposure to fast-growing consumer and tech brands

 - Mentorship from experienced Creative Strategists and Creative Producer

 - Collaborative in-office environment in NYC

 - Potential for full-time opportunities based on performance

source_jobs.title: Static ad designer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT
NewForm is the creative intelligence company. We help category disruptors like Kalshi, ElevenLabs, Babbel, and others scale paid ads by turning creative into a measurable, repeatable growth system.

We oversee $100M+ per year in paid media spend and create thousands of human ads each month, with each ad making our system smarter.

THE ROLE

As a Static Ad Designer, you’ll be responsible for designing high-performing static ads for paid social channels, primarily Meta. You’ll work closely with creative strategists, media buyers, and account teams to turn concepts into scroll-stopping assets that drive measurable results.

This role is ideal for a designer who moves fast, thinks in systems, and is excited by performance feedback. You’ll be producing at high volume, iterating quickly based on data, and scale accounts with your assets.

RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design static ads for paid social (primarily Meta: Facebook & Instagram)

 - Translate creative concepts into clear, compelling visual executions

 - Produce multiple variations per concept to support testing and iteration

 - Collaborate with creative strategists to refine layouts and messaging

 - Iterate on designs based on performance data and feedback

 - Maintain brand consistency while adapting designs for performance needs

 - Organize and manage design files for efficient handoff and reuse

 - Stay current on paid social design trends and best practices

REQUIREMENTS

 - 2–5 years of graphic design experience (performance marketing or agency experience preferred)

 - Strong portfolio demonstrating static ad design for paid social

 - Proficiency in Figma, Photoshop, and/or Illustrator

 - Ability to design quickly without sacrificing clarity or quality

 - Strong understanding of hierarchy, typography, and composition

 - Strong attention to detail and organization

 - (Must) Experience designing ads for DTC, mobile apps, or SaaS

 - (Must) Familiarity with Meta ad specs and formats

BENEFITS

 - Full-time, remote role

 - Competitive compensation based on experience

 - Performance-based bonus

 - Annual team offsites (previous trips include Tulum, Mexico and Hokkaido, Japan)

source_jobs.title: User acquistion specialist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT
NewForm is the creative intelligence company. We help category disruptors like Kalshi, ElevenLabs, Babbel, and others scale paid ads by turning creative into a measurable, repeatable growth system.

We oversee $100M+ per year in paid media spend and create thousands of human ads each month, with each ad making our system smarter.


THE ROLE:
As a user acquisition specialist, you will play a key role in managing paid social campaigns on Meta, Tiktok and Google across multiple clients. You’ll own campaign execution, testing, and optimization, working with creative strategists and growth marketing managers to drive client outcomes. You will strategically leverage our proprietary technology to unlock wins and scale media budgets efficiently

RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own end-to-end media management: campaign setup, targeting, bidding, budgets, creative rotation, and optimization.

 - Monitor and improve key metrics: CPI/CAC, ROAS, payback, retention.

 - Diagnose performance shifts (creative fatigue, audience changes, funnel changes, attribution issues) and take action quickly.

 - Maintain account hygiene: naming conventions, UTM/parameter integrity, QA checks, and documentation.

 - Proactively suggest optimizations to improve clients' media program efficiency and
   scalability

REQUIREMENTS

 - 2-4 years of paid media experience (agency AND/OR consumer app experience highly
   preferred). Expertise in Meta required.

 - Experience managing accounts with 7-figure annual media budgets.

 - Strong presentation skills: the ability to craft compelling decks and speak effectively in
   client meetings is a must

 - Strong analytical skills

 - (Preferred) A deep understanding of mobile advertising (SKAN, AEM, MMPs, etc)
   

BENEFITS

 - This is a remote international position; applicants are encouraged to apply from anywhere. Our working hours are EST (office in NYC).

 - Competitive compensation based on geo

 - Competitive bonus

 - Annual team offsites (last two were Tulum, Mexico and Hokkaido, Japan)

notes: NewForm is a creative intelligence company that helps category disruptors scale paid ads. They have a strong focus on creative production and paid social campaigns.
